title: HCL Syntax and Terraform Basics
learningObjectives: Understand HCL syntax: blocks, arguments, and expressions
Run terraform init, plan, apply, and destroy
Understand the Terraform 1.13 workflow and new CLI commands

theory.content:
What is Terraform?

Terraform by HashiCorp is an Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tool that lets you define, provision, and manage cloud infrastructure using HCL (HashiCorp Configuration Language). Terraform 1.13 is the current N-1 release (latest is 1.14) and brings the terraform stacks CLI command to GA, a stable terraform rpcapi, and improved test variable definitions.

HCL Syntax

# Block syntax
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami           = "ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0"
  instance_type = "t3.micro"

  tags = {
    Name        = "web-server"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Variables with validation (stable since 1.9+)
variable "environment" {
  type        = string
  description = "Deployment environment"
  default     = "dev"
  validation {
    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "prod"], var.environment)
    error_message = "Environment must be dev, staging, or prod."
  }
}

# Locals
locals {
  common_tags = {
    Project   = "myapp"
    ManagedBy = "Terraform"
  }
}

# Outputs
output "instance_ip" {
  value       = aws_instance.web.public_ip
  description = "Public IP of the web instance"
}

Core Terraform Workflow

  1. terraform init — Download providers, initialize backend, install modules
  2. terraform plan — Show what changes will be made (dry run, no modifications)
  3. terraform apply — Apply the plan (creates/modifies/destroys resources)
  4. terraform destroy — Destroy all resources managed by this configuration

What is New in Terraform 1.13?

  • terraform stacks (GA): The terraform stacks CLI command is now generally available. Stacks allow composing multiple Terraform configurations into a single deployable unit with shared state coordination — ideal for multi-component platforms.
  • terraform rpcapi (GA): Exposes certain Terraform operations through an RPC interface compatible with go-plugin, enabling programmatic integration with Terraform from external tools.
  • Improved terraform test: External variables referenced in .tftest.hcl files should now have an accompanying variable definition block in the test file itself, making complex variable passing more explicit and less error-prone.
  • Bug fixes: Fixes for test run parallelism deadlock and resource identity being dropped from state in edge cases.

Required Version Constraint

terraform {
  required_version = "~> 1.13"
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }
}
